channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nigerian military faces casualties during high-risk hostage rescue operation
item.description: The article discusses a recent incident where Nigerian military personnel encountered casualties while attempting to rescue hostages during a critical operation. Despite the risks involved, the missio...

item.title: Legal Dispute Over Mlimani City Mall Ownership Raises Concerns About Foreign Investment in Tanzania
item.description: A Botswana-based company is embroiled in a legal dispute over the ownership of Mlimani City Mall, one of Tanzania's premier retail destinations in Dar es Salaam. The controversy highlights complex own...

item.title: The Crisis of Traditional Leadership in South Africa: Accountability in the Digital Age
item.description: The article critiques South Africa's failure to regulate traditional leadership in the digital age, using the Zulu monarchy's scandals as a case study. It argues that the monarchy, funded by the state...

item.title: Ogun State Water Corp Addresses Abeokuta Water Disruption Caused by Road Projects
item.description: The Ogun State Water Corporation (OGSWC) has explained that water supply issues in parts of Abeokuta are due to damage to pipelines during road construction projects by the previous administration, no...

item.title: 25 young black British actors and actresses making waves in film and TV
item.description: The article highlights 25 young Black British actors and actresses who are gaining recognition in the entertainment industry. These talents are making significant impacts in film, television, and thea...

item.title: Transport Canada oversees safety measures after train derailment near Repentigny
item.description: Transport Canada has appointed an observer to oversee the investigation into a train derailment that occurred northeast of Montreal, near the residential area of Repentigny, Quebec. The incident, whic...

item.title: Springboks Triumph Over Scotland in Pretoria Nations Championship Match
item.description: The South African national rugby team, the Springboks, secured a decisive 42-28 victory against Scotland in their Nations Championship clash at Loftus Versfeld. Despite being labeled inexperienced, th...

item.title: Legendary playback singer S. Janaki passes away after a legendary career
item.description: S. Janaki, a legendary playback singer known for her versatility across South Indian cinema, has passed away. Her career spanned decades, with iconic songs that blended classical music with film melod...

item.title: John Steinbeck's 'East of Eden' and Its Enduring Literary Controversy
item.description: The article explores the enduring debate surrounding John Steinbeck's novel 'East of Eden,' published in 1939, which has remained a subject of critical discussion. It delves into the novel's themes of...

item.title: England prepare for World Cup quarter-final against Norway with tactical focus and key player updates
item.description: The England national team is set to face Norway in the FIFA World Cup quarter-final, with manager Thomas Tuchel emphasizing strategic preparation ahead of the match. The game will take place under cha...

item.title: FDA Advises Caution Against Counterfeit Dengue Vaccines
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued a warning to the public against the purchase and use of counterfeit versions of the dengue vaccine QdengaTM. The agency clarified that the QdengaTM De...

item.title: Access Issues: News Corp Australia Blocks Bot Traffic
item.description: The article details an error message encountered by users attempting to access News Corp Australia websites, indicating they may have been detected as a crawler bot. News Corp Australia employs softwa...

item.title: Kamloops Liquor Store Employee Sexually Assaulted: RCMP Investigating
item.description: A Vancouver-based employee was sexually assaulted at a liquor store in Kamloops, British Columbia, according to RCMP reports. The incident, which occurred on July 11, 2026, has prompted a formal inves...

item.title: Certificate in Nursing in Kenya: Requirements, Colleges, and Fees
item.description: The article provides comprehensive information on pursuing a Certificate in Nursing in Kenya. It outlines the entry requirements, including a minimum KCSE mean grade of C- and specific subject combina...

item.title: How Music Powered Sudan’s 2019 Revolution
item.description: The article explores the pivotal role of music in Sudan's 2019 revolution, highlighting how it became a unifying force for protesters. It discusses how music cultivated anti-government sentiment and s...

item.title: Justin Bone's Mental State and RCMP Interaction Before Chinatown Murders
item.description: The article details the video evidence presented during the second-degree murder trial of Justin Bone, accused of the 2022 killings of Ban Phuc Hoang and Hung Trang in Edmonton. The 49-minute video, r...

item.title: Pitbull's Children and Family Life: Privacy and Protection
item.description: The article discusses the personal life of rapper Pitbull, focusing on his six children. It highlights how he maintains privacy for his family, revealing that two of his kids have public profiles whil...

item.title: Jennifer Garner's New Series Explores Grief, Friendship, and Culinary Healing
item.description: In 'The Five-Star Weekend,' Jennifer Garner portrays Hollis Shaw, a food blogger navigating grief by inviting friends to her Nantucket summer home. The series delves into themes of friendship, self-pr...

item.title: JMFyang aids Songbayanan's victory in 'Singko Blanko' contest

item.title: Venezuela Earthquakes: Over 4,300 Dead, 19,000 in Shelters
item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes last month has surpassed 4,300, according to National Assembly President Jorge Rodriguez. The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes on June 24 flatt...
